pi_items.source: Found in setup_task.sh and verifier.py where the source rocket file is downloaded and verified from.

Inferred from task description (0.65 g/cm³ = 650 kg/m³) and confirmed in verifier.py which looks for this value within density_tolerance_kgm3.

Confirmed in verifier.py which awards points if max_ballast_mass >= min_ballast.
Confirmed in verifier.py which requires the score to be >= pass_threshold to mark the task as passed.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a non-synthetic OpenRocket design file ('janus_29mm.ork') downloaded from the '3dp-rocket' GitHub repository. The required material density modification (0.65 g/cm³) corresponds precisely to a target density of 650 kg/m³ in the .ork XML configuration. The evaluation logic strictly enforces the addition of a ballast (mass component) with a minimum mass of 0.01 kg (10 grams) inside the nose cone. Note that the original .ork file contains explicit `<overridemass>` elements for its main structural components, so modifying their material density will not automatically recalculate their physical mass or affect the simulated stability margin unless the mass overrides are also removed.
